name: interrogation-room
summary: Built for questions, not entertainment. A chair, a table, a light aimed at the prisoner — cleaner and more focused than a torture chamber.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: compact rectangle — the room focuses on a single chair

primary_palette.note: classic setup — prisoner in shackled chair, interrogator across the table
lone chair under harsh light, implements within reach
civil interrogation — the table is the weapon, no visible restraints
arcane interrogation — truth by magical means
bureaucratic interrogator — a clerk with ledgers and a chained subject

story_prompts: mid-interrogation — papers on the table, the chair still warm
immaculately clean, recently scrubbed, all evidence removed
inquisitorial chamber with a confession already written, awaiting the signature
a wizard's interrogation room — a magic circle, a single chair, no physical implements
improvised interrogation in a closet — a chair dragged in, a lantern on the floor

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim with one bright focal source (0.15-0.3)

anti_patterns: don't confuse with torture-chamber — interrogation is minimalist, not cluttered
don't light evenly — one bright source on the prisoner, rest in shadow
avoid ornamentation; this room is about discomfort, not aesthetic
